originalTitle: Oracle beats on earnings, but stock drops on plans to raise another $20 billion
summary: Oracle topped quarterly expectations, but free cash flow is negative, and the company plans to raise more capital for data center projects.
content:
Oracle reported better-than-expected earnings and revenue for the fiscal fourth quarter on Wednesday while also raising its profit forecast for the year. The stock dropped 10% in extended trading as the company plans to raise more money to finance its AI buildout.

Here's how the company did in comparison with LSEG consensus:

Revenue increased 21% year over year in the quarter, which ended on May 31, according to a statement . Net income rose to $4.22 billion, or $1.45 per share, from $3.43 billion, or $1.19 per share, a year ago. Adjusted earnings exclude impact of stock-based compensation.

The company maintained its previous revenue guidance of $90 billion for the 2027 fiscal year, while lifting its forecast of adjusted earnings per share to $8.05. Analysts were projecting $8.01 per share and $88.9 billion in revenue.

Oracle said it foresees raising $40 billion through debt and equity financing, including a $20 billion share sale it announced earlier. That's after raising $43 billion in debt and $5 billion in equity in fiscal 2026, a move that concerned investors due to uncertainty about whether demand for artificial intelligence can justify that much new capital.

For the fiscal year, Oracle reported $23.7 billion in negative free cash flow, with depreciation nearly doubling to $7.62 billion. Capital expenditures jumped 162% to $55.7 billion.

The company called for $1.72 to $1.76 in adjusted earnings per share for the fiscal first quarter, with 27% to 29% revenue growth. Analysts polled by LSEG had been expecting $1.68 in adjusted earnings per share, along with $19.06 billion in revenue, implying about 28% growth.

Revenue from cloud offerings increased 47% in the quarter to $9.91 billion. Analysts polled by StreetAccount had expected $9.97 billion. Software revenue, including licenses and support, totaled $6.82 billion, down 2% but above StreetAccount's $6.93 billion consensus.

Cloud infrastructure revenue jumped 93% to $5.8 billion. Amazon Web Services, the leader in the market, generated $37.59 billion in revenue in the March quarter.

Oracle's remaining performance obligation, including revenue that hasn't been recognized, reached $638 billion on May 31, up 363%. Analysts polled by StreetAccount had been looking for $595.67 billion.

"Most of the RPO increase in both Q3 and Q4 were large scale AI contracts where the customer prepaid Oracle for the purchase of the GPUs, or the customer bought and supplied the GPUs to Oracle," the company said in the statement, referring to graphics processing units.

The two mechanisms lower the sum Oracle will need for data center construction, the company said.

Bank of America analysts, who recommend buying Oracle shares, said over 50% of the remaining performance obligation comes from OpenAI.

The company is looking to bring online almost one gigawatt worth of computing power in the current quarter, roughly the total for fiscal 2026, Oracle CEO Clay Magouyrk said on a conference call with analysts.

During the quarter, Oracle hired Schneider Electric executive Hilary Maxson to be its new chief financial officer . Related Digital and Blackstone said they secured funding for a $16 billion Oracle data center site in Michigan.

Oracle's net cash outlay for capital expenditures in fiscal 2027 will be around $70 billion, excluding $20 billion to $25 billion in prepayments from customers and timing impact, Maxson said.

As of Wednesday's close, the stock was up 3% so far in 2026, while the S&P 500 had gained 6% in the same period.

WATCH: Cramer’s Stop Trading: Oracle
chineseTitle: 甲骨文盈利好于预期，但因计划再融资 200 亿美元而股价下跌
chineseSummary: 甲骨文季度业绩超出预期，但自由现金流为负，该公司计划为数据中心项目筹集更多资金。
chineseContent:
甲骨文周三公布的第四财季盈利和收入好于预期，同时还上调了今年的利润预期。由于该公司计划筹集更多资金为其人工智能建设提供资金，该股在盘后交易中下跌了 10%。

以下是该公司与 LSEG 共识的比较结果：

一份声明称，截至 5 月 31 日的季度收入同比增长 21%。净利润从一年前的 34.3 亿美元（即每股 1.19 美元）增至 42.2 亿美元（即每股 1.45 美元）。调整后收益不包括股票薪酬的影响。

该公司维持之前 2027 财年 900 亿美元的收入指引，同时将调整后每股收益预测上调至 8.05 美元。分析师预计每股收益为 8.01 美元，收入为 889 亿美元。

甲骨文表示，预计通过债务和股权融资筹集 400 亿美元资金，其中包括早些时候宣布的 200 亿美元股票出售。这是在 2026 财年筹集了 430 亿美元的债务和 50 亿美元的股本之后，此举引起了投资者的担忧，因为不确定人工智能的需求是否足以证明这么多新资本的合理性。

本财年，甲骨文公布的自由现金流为负 237 亿美元，折旧几乎翻倍，达到 76.2 亿美元。资本支出跃升 162%，达到 557 亿美元。

该公司预计第一财季调整后每股收益为1.72至1.76美元，收入增长27%至29%。伦敦证券交易所集团 (LSEG) 调查的分析师此前预计调整后每股收益为 1.68 美元，营收为 190.6 亿美元，意味着增长约 28%。

本季度云产品收入增长 47%，达到 99.1 亿美元。 StreetAccount 调查的分析师此前预期为 99.7 亿美元。软件收入（包括许可和支持）总计 68.2 亿美元，下降 2%，但高于 StreetAccount 69.3 亿美元的共识。

云基础设施收入跃升 93%，达到 58 亿美元。市场领先者亚马逊网络服务在 3 月份季度创造了 375.9 亿美元的收入。

截至 5 月 31 日，甲骨文的剩余履约义务（包括尚未确认的收入）达到 6380 亿美元，增长了 363%。 StreetAccount 调查的分析师预计该公司的估值为 5,956.7 亿美元。

该公司在声明中提到图形处理单元时表示：“第三季度和第四季度的 RPO 增长大部分来自大规模人工智能合同，其中客户预付甲骨文购买 GPU 的费用，或者客户购买并向甲骨文供应 GPU。”

该公司表示，这两种机制降低了甲骨文数据中心建设所需的资金。

建议购买甲骨文股票的美国银行分析师表示，剩余履约义务的 50% 以上来自 OpenAI。

甲骨文首席执行官 Clay Magouyrk 在与分析师举行的电话会议上表示，该公司希望在本季度实现近 1 吉瓦的在线计算能力，大致相当于 2026 财年的总计算能力。

本季度，甲骨文聘请施耐德电气高管希拉里·马克森（Hilary Maxson）担任其新任首席财务官。 Related Digital 和 Blackstone 表示，他们已为密歇根州价值 160 亿美元的 Oracle 数据中心站点获得融资。

Maxson 表示，甲骨文 2027 财年资本支出的净现金支出将约为 700 亿美元，不包括客户预付款和时间影响的 200 亿至 250 亿美元。

截至周三收盘，该股 2026 年迄今已上涨 3%，而同期标准普尔 500 指数已上涨 6%。

观看：克莱默的停止交易：甲骨文
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
